primary_reason: This systematic review combines heterogeneous human and animal infection data through standardized geographic units and pooled prevalence estimates, providing a transferable zoonotic-burden synthesis design.
This study combines questionnaires, passive case surveillance, active longitudinal surveillance, and a nested microbiome substudy to measure travelers' diarrhea and its consequences.
This paper proposes real-time changes in the naive case-fatality ratio as a diagnostic signal of delayed case confirmation and infectious-disease under-ascertainment.
This Chilean COVID-19 analysis jointly tracks reproduction numbers, underreporting, diagnostic effort, fatality, and intensive-care capacity to evaluate interventions and system saturation.
This paper diagnoses identifiability problems in a SEIRD model and uses Monte Carlo experiments to evaluate estimators that accommodate case underreporting.
This cross-country analysis adjusts for reporting and socioeconomic differences while estimating lockdown effects on COVID-19 deaths and the economic value implicitly assigned to lives saved.
The study demonstrates public-genome screening for longitudinal surveillance of antimicrobial resistance genes and predicted phenotypes.
This review concerns general adverse-drug-reaction reporting and does not provide an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete route for transferring its findings to infectious surveillance.

This systematic review pools infection-eradication, recurrence, healing, and amputation outcomes across treatment strategies for fracture-related infection while identifying classification and reporting limitations.
This single urinary-tract-infection case describes an unusual pathogen and its treatment without contributing a reusable epidemiologic or surveillance design.
This review critically evaluates coronavirus air-pollution studies, identifies ecological, temporal, spatial, and confounding biases, and prescribes stronger longitudinal designs.

This synthesis of African national tuberculosis prevalence surveys extracts practical lessons about population screening, radiography, notification gaps, and comparison of prevalence with routine case reports.
This review evaluates evidence and knowledge gaps for vampire-bat rabies surveillance and control and outlines candidate technologies and management priorities.
This review explains the tools, limitations, underreporting problems, and communication requirements of surveillance for adverse events following immunization.
This article advocates preserving dengue-control resources during COVID-19 but does not develop or evaluate a surveillance, analytic, or policy-evaluation method.
This single malaria case documents spontaneous splenic rupture without a transferable epidemiologic or measurement contribution.
This paper introduces a Bayesian SIR formulation with underreporting correction and uses simulated reporting scenarios to examine effects on epidemic estimates.
This study extends an SEIR model for diagnostic misclassification and test-selection bias, evaluates it through simulations, and supplies an R package.
This study estimates passive vaccine-safety surveillance sensitivity by comparing VAERS reporting rates with independent Vaccine Safety Datalink incidence estimates.
This review assesses field performance, validation, regulation, and implementation barriers for point-of-care surveillance of infectious and zoonotic animal diseases in low-resource settings.
This VAERS case-series description of vocal-fold immobility reports temporal associations without a reusable surveillance-adjustment or causal design.
This preparedness review provides concrete recommendations for epidemic funds, surveillance units, secure historical databases, and strategic outbreak-data analysis in African countries.
This study links notification, microbiology, and hospital-discharge sources and applies three-source capture-recapture analysis to estimate invasive pneumococcal disease underreporting.
This study combines delay-adjusted fatality ratios with a Bayesian Gaussian-process model to reconstruct time-varying COVID-19 case ascertainment and validate estimates against serosurveys.
This cross-sectional regression reports demographic and geographic associations with national COVID-19 case counts but does not identify a reusable design beyond ordinary regression.
This clinical review concerns a noninfectious inflammatory skin disorder and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This narrative overview summarizes rabies burden and control in Nepal without substantively evaluating or prescribing a transferable epidemiologic method.
This paper develops a spatial Bayesian hierarchical model that integrates linked passive and active surveillance data to estimate and correct spatially varying under-ascertainment.
This cross-sectional resident survey describes needlestick frequency, reporting, and perceived blood-borne infection risk without a reusable analytic or measurement contribution.
This study uses the WHO Data Quality Self-assessment tool to audit discrepancies between facility immunization registers and reports submitted to a health directorate.
This study uses legally mandated coroner reports, historical baselines, and excess-mortality calculations to evaluate COVID-19 deaths and near-real-time mortality surveillance.
This validation study surveys parents of children missing from a vaccination register to distinguish true nonvaccination from unrecorded vaccination and correct coverage estimates.
This letter argues that racialized social and biological vulnerabilities require investigation but presents no method, diagnostic analysis, or reporting framework.
This study combines a population survey with sentinel-hospital surveillance and a multiplier model to estimate underdiagnosed foodborne Salmonella and Vibrio disease burdens.
This comparison concerns assisted-reproduction registries and does not state a concrete route for transferring its findings to infectious-disease surveillance.
This study develops decomposable transmission metrics and a dynamic-panel estimation framework intended to supplement contaminated or incomplete COVID-19 surveillance counts.
This five-patient histoplasmosis case series highlights diagnostic recognition in transplant recipients without contributing a transferable epidemiologic method.
The article describes a malaria neurological case and summarizes clinical presentations without contributing a transferable epidemiological method.
This meta-review pools narcolepsy incidence and prevalence across countries and vaccination periods while assessing underrepresentation by demographic group.
This paper derives and validates an analytic real-time probability of epidemic elimination and formally evaluates sensitivity to underreporting and imported-case misclassification.
This dengue cost-of-illness analysis combines a validated cost-prediction model, human-capital estimates, and an underreporting expansion factor.
This study critiques Mexico's sentinel model by using reproduction-number-based corrections to estimate unidentified COVID-19 cases and deaths.
This paper develops a hidden-state count model that embeds SIR epidemic dynamics and time-varying underreporting within a nonstationary Poisson autoregressive process.
This methodological review compares field, modeling, outbreak-investigation, and mitigation evidence to guide surveillance and recognition of airborne transmission across spatial scales.
This broad clinical and veterinary overview summarizes fascioliasis epidemiology, diagnosis, treatment, and control without a substantive methods comparison or reusable design.
The review evaluates approved Zika serological tests, cross-reactivity limitations, and antibody technologies central to diagnostic surveillance.
This chapter substantively explains European Legionnaires' disease surveillance definitions, organizational responsibilities, reporting methods, outputs, and remaining ascertainment problems.
This study evaluates two denominator-based congenital Zika surveillance measures, uses discordance to identify underreporting, and recommends improved future reporting.
The title identifies cross-reactivity in a CDC SARS-CoV-2 specimen control as a source of false-negative results and infection underreporting.
This study combines an importation simulation with spatial transmissibility estimates to locate areas likely to have experienced cryptic Zika spread.
This paper develops and fits an extended SEIRD model that estimates time-varying ascertainment, infection fatality, interventions, and future COVID-19 transmission.
This structured review extracts pathogens, settings, and control measures from African healthcare-associated infection outbreaks and diagnoses systematic under-detection and reporting gaps.
This longitudinal HIV study estimates cumulative-incidence contrasts while accounting for time-varying confounding, competing events, and missing data.
This study links traveler importation risk to the probability of local dengue transmission to produce location- and time-specific outbreak forecasts.
This case series primarily concerns allergy to polyethylene glycol across consumer and medical products and does not evaluate an infectious outcome or infectious-surveillance method.
This study derives underreporting multipliers from repeated seroprevalence surveys, validates them externally, and applies them to estimate multiple components of national COVID-19 burden.
This paper introduces a two-distribution time-series mixture model for detecting and quantifying underreporting in longitudinal genital-wart incidence data.
This study links hospital administrative and laboratory records, validates morbidity-code sensitivity against laboratory diagnoses, and estimates Campylobacter hospitalization rates.
This review critically assesses dengue disease and vector surveillance and recommends integrated sentinel, adult-mosquito, and climate-based early-warning systems.
This paper analytically demonstrates pre-peak epidemic-model underidentification and explains how inconsistent testing and reporting corrupt parameter estimation.
This cross-sectional survey describes needlestick prevalence and reporting behavior using standard comparisons without a transferable surveillance-adjustment contribution.
The title suggests a distinction between asymptomatic bacteriuria and symptom underreporting, but the absent abstract leaves the study's methods and contribution indeterminate.
This study applies delay-adjusted case-fatality and test-positivity methods to produce time-varying COVID-19 reporting fractions across Indian states.
This malaria study audits reporting completeness while integrating facility records, spatial patterns, temporal trends, and meteorological covariates for elimination planning.
This rapid review compares Iranian COVID-19 estimation models, documents reporting heterogeneity, and recommends minimum reporting items and explicit treatment of underreporting.
This preprint reports the same World Mortality Dataset, authors, excess-mortality analysis, and central findings as the subsequently published retained record PMID:34190045.
This study develops and externally validates a VAERS-based prediction model for post-vaccination Guillain-Barre syndrome while assessing the effect of selectively underreported features.
This population serosurvey estimates SARS-CoV-2 prevalence, undetected-infection ratios, and infection fatality in an underserved urban settlement.
This surveillance study uses blinded recruitment, multiple population-sampling routes, adjusted estimation, and formal bias analyses to estimate county-level SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence.
This systematic review maps historical human and intermediate-host Paragonimus occurrences to identify stable endemic foci, host differences, and geographic sampling gaps.
This clinical review summarizes neurologic manifestations and treatment issues in acute HIV infection without a transferable epidemiologic or measurement contribution.
This study compares electronic health records with death certificates to quantify omission of hepatitis C among decedents with advanced liver disease.
This paper develops and cross-validates a local incidence-adjustment method that combines diagnostic-stockout corrections, healthcare-access indices, benchmark multipliers, and spatial pooling.
This study uses a Bayesian missing-data model to impute occupation and infecting serovar in partially complete leptospirosis notifications and inform intervention targeting.
This multi-state regression analysis tests whether reduced norovirus outbreaks are better explained by pandemic interventions than by seasonality or surveillance underreporting.
This psychiatric case concerns a false belief of parasitic infestation rather than an infection and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This study combines regression and machine-learning distribution models into a fine-scale ensemble map for targeting Buruli-ulcer surveillance beyond known endemic areas.
This audit links facility records to two national tuberculosis systems and evaluates underreporting, timeliness, concordance, accuracy, and completeness.
This study combines pediatric serology with vaccination histories and disease notifications to assess immunity persistence and infer varicella underreporting.
This mini-review advances a speculative immunological hypothesis about malaria and COVID-19 without testing it or providing a substantive epidemiologic method.
This retrospective notification review describes workers experiencing biological-material accidents but does not develop a reusable surveillance or correction method.

This paper designs and evaluates a hybrid tuberculosis inventory method combining record digitization, deterministic and probabilistic linkage, facility census, stratified sampling, extrapolation, and qualitative inquiry.
This paper details a proportional redistribution procedure for reallocating ill-defined causes of death, including lower respiratory infections, in burden-of-disease analyses.
This study combines event detection with seasonal time-series baselines of severe respiratory infection to estimate state-specific COVID-19 case and death underreporting.
This representative household serosurvey adjusts for sampling design and assay performance and uses mixed models and permutation tests to evaluate SARS-CoV-2 prevalence and clustering.
This pharmacy-sales validation study measures injection-equipment access and opioid-related needs without observing an infectious outcome or specifying an infectious-surveillance transfer.
This review concerns harmful algal blooms and toxin-related ecological and health events rather than infectious disease, with no concrete infectious transfer route.
This paper develops a state-space compartmental model separating detected and undetected infections and estimates its parameters through Bayesian Gibbs sampling.
This convenience-sample survey describes quarantine attitudes and self-reported preventive practices using standard cross-sectional associations without a reusable design contribution.
This study links hospitalization and mortality records and applies cause-of-death coding rules to identify and correct underreported AIDS deaths.
This clinical review addresses a noninfectious inflammatory skin condition and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease methods.
This paper develops and validates machine-learning classifiers for detecting suspected foodborne outbreaks and uses feature-attribution methods to identify confounding factors.
This systematic review identifies molecularly confirmed duodenoscope outbreaks and estimates minimum infection and colonization risks using national procedure denominators.
This AMR study directly compares clinical, epidemiological, and livestock-specific susceptibility interpretation thresholds and demonstrates their differing classification consequences.

This expert review identifies structural barriers to surgical-site-infection surveillance and recommends standardized definitions, reporting methods, accountability, training, and regional information exchange.
This computable phenotype identifies transgender and gender-nonconforming patients, while sexually transmitted infections are only a secondary observed disparity rather than the method's target.
This qualitative interview study examines how outbreak-control policies, camp conditions, healthcare access, and community distrust affect transmission prevention and COVID-19 reporting.
This integrated human serologic and mosquito molecular investigation detects co-circulating flaviviruses missed by official surveillance.
This prospective community cohort uses weekly active follow-up and multiple laboratory assays to estimate symptomatic dengue incidence despite routine misdiagnosis and underreporting.
This paper incorporates time-varying false-negative testing into an SEIR model and validates estimated hidden infections against repeated serosurveys.
This three-case series and clinical review describes varicella-zoster meningitis without a reusable epidemiologic, diagnostic-validation, or surveillance design.
This paper describes elevated COVID-19 mortality among African political leaders and discusses possible explanations without establishing a transferable analytic design.
This study triangulates direct adjustments, excess respiratory mortality, excess natural-cause mortality, testing corrections, and lasso prediction to estimate unreported COVID-19 deaths.
This ecological analysis uses quasi-Poisson generalized additive models to estimate expected respiratory deaths and characterize regional and temporal COVID-19 excess mortality.
This study compares four government immunization-spending reporting systems using ratios, trends, underreporting estimates, and concordance analyses.
This systematic review synthesizes sexually transmitted infection and other reproductive-health outcomes associated with temporary marriage in a stigmatized and underreported population.
This review summarizes reptilian herpesvirus taxonomy, pathology, and epidemiology without substantively comparing or developing surveillance or analytic methods.
This study reviews electronic clinical records and compares adjudicated cardiac-device infections with registry reports to quantify surveillance underreporting.
This systematic review and meta-analysis compares self-reported HIV status with biological or clinical knowledge proxies to quantify subgroup-specific underreporting.
This paper develops an integrated statistical framework for normalizing epidemic cycles and estimating reproduction, undernotification, onset, duration, and total infections.
This study relates changes in funding, policy, and testing strategy to shifts in South Sudan's surveillance sources, yields, and likely COVID-19 underreporting.
This review audits race and ethnicity reporting and representation across first-wave COVID-19 studies, identifying systematic omissions that limit interpretation and reproducibility.
The title concerns reporting of genetic variants causing a noninfectious syndrome and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This review provides testing, follow-up, treatment, and prevention guidance for HIV-syphilis coinfection while identifying pandemic-related surveillance uncertainty.
The study applies a cross-sectional rapid-antibody serosurvey to estimate underascertained SARS-CoV-2 infection and time-varying serologic detection.
This study compares forecasts, uncertainty, and underreporting estimates from five statistical and mechanistic COVID-19 models against held-out Indian surveillance data.
This retrospective malaria report presents counts, maps, ordinary correlations, and spatial clustering among miners without a reusable measurement or causal-design contribution.
The study uses serologic measurement and multivariable analysis to assess vaccination response, susceptibility, circulation, and underreporting.
This systematic review and meta-analysis estimates regional vulvovaginal candidiasis prevalence and explicitly evaluates geographic data gaps and likely under-ascertainment.
This study demonstrates an enhanced hospital-based system for estimating infant pertussis incidence and clinical burden beyond routine surveillance.
This paper develops an intervention-aware SIRD model with an explicit underreporting observation process and counterfactual policy simulations.
This Iranian application triangulates phylogenetics, exported-case travel data, excess mortality, and an SEIR model to reconstruct an underreported epidemic and evaluate interventions.
This study develops a quantitative air-travel transmission-risk estimator combining documented index cases, passenger denominators, asymptomatic-infection corrections, and underreporting uncertainty.
This study compares negative-binomial mixed-model formulations using proper scoring rules to characterize age- and region-specific heterogeneity in norovirus reporting.
This computational method-comparison study uses numerical experiments to show how observation-function misspecification and neglected underreporting bias ensemble Kalman filtering of epidemic models.
This published resource provides an openly available, frequently updated international mortality dataset and a reproducible excess-mortality approach for correcting pandemic death comparisons.

This report compares notified tuberculosis and influenza counts before and during COVID-19 but cannot distinguish underreporting, misclassification, and true incidence changes through a reusable design.
This structured review describes the clinical characteristics of 83 pediatric histoplasmosis cases without a transferable epidemiologic or surveillance method.
The study uses Monte Carlo uncertainty simulation to compare age-specific vaccine-preventable deaths against vaccine-associated mortality risks.
The article develops a general two-strain infectious-disease model for estimating mutant takeover and evaluating vaccination and interventions.
This One Health analysis integrates two multinational rabies databases, spatial clustering, source attribution, and mixed models to examine cross-species dynamics.
This multinational mechanistic model integrates behavior, asymptomatic transmission, disease severity, hospital capacity, underreporting, vaccination, and counterfactual adherence scenarios.
This nationally representative, age-stratified household serosurvey uses probability sampling and survey weights to quantify SARS-CoV-2 under-ascertainment in Sierra Leone.
This study combines national serosurveys with a compartmental epidemic model to estimate wave-specific hidden infections, deaths, and infection-fatality risks in India.
This study combines longitudinal Lassa-fever presentation records with a community survey to evaluate persistent changes in healthcare seeking after Ebola.
This paper develops a simple age-structured correction model that combines reported prevalence with relative contact rates to estimate unregistered infections early in a pandemic.
This validation compares international opioid-availability statistics with Scandinavian sales data and does not provide an infectious outcome or concrete infectious-surveillance transfer route.
This study describes tick-borne encephalitis notification trends before and during COVID-19 but offers only speculative explanations for the observed decline.
This pharmacovigilance guidance explains why spontaneous vaccine-report counts and disproportionality signals require clinical review, background rates, and follow-up epidemiologic studies.
This metapopulation application models how internal mobility, border migration, interventions, and fuel shortages shaped SARS-CoV-2 spread across Venezuela.
The study uses an age-structured decision model to evaluate dengue vaccination, prevaccination screening, underreporting, costs, and affordability.
This synthetic-data study generates patient-level records from registry summary statistics and evaluates machine-learning prediction of COVID-19 outcomes among people with inflammatory bowel disease.
This review combines historical literature with updated health-ministry surveillance data to assess meningococcal trends, serogroup gaps, and immunization strategy in Saudi Arabia.
This living Cochrane review applies formal risk-of-bias and GRADE methods to synthesize randomized evidence on systemic corticosteroids for COVID-19.
This systematic review uses random-effects logistic meta-regression to estimate severe yellow-fever fatality risk and assess heterogeneity across settings and data sources.
This theoretical transmission study models how sub-spreading heterogeneity and selective underreporting affect epidemic fade-out and the reliability of elimination declarations.
This paper constructs national and state correction factors for incomplete death registration and cause certification and recommends explicit labeling of reported versus estimated COVID-19 deaths.
This review concerns medication-induced constipation and provides no infectious outcome or concrete route to infectious-disease methodology.
This study uses a spatial Bayesian hierarchical model and grade-2 disability as a delayed-diagnosis proxy to estimate microregional leprosy underreporting.
This nationally representative pediatrician survey diagnoses knowledge, testing, and reporting practices that can produce mumps underdiagnosis and delayed outbreak response.
